# @zakkster/lite-headless/drawer

Slide-in panel anchored to a viewport edge. Like a dialog whose
visual anchor is an edge instead of the center -- composes the same
overlay machinery (portal, focus trap, scroll lock, escape + outside
dismiss).

Use for: filter panels, mobile nav, row-detail side panes, settings
drawers, anything that "slides in from the edge".

## API

```js
import { createDrawer } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/drawer";

const drawer = createDrawer({
    defaultOpen: false,
    defaultSide: "right",            // "left" | "right" | "top" | "bottom"
    modal: true,                      // backdrop + focus trap + scroll lock
    closeOnEscape: true,
    closeOnOutsideClick: true,
    lockScrollOnOpen: true,
    trapFocus: true,
    returnFocus: true,
    awaitTransitionEnd: false,        // wait for slide-out before flipping to closed
    onOpenChange: (open, reason) => {},
});

drawer.attachContent(asideEl);
drawer.attachTrigger(buttonEl);
drawer.attachBackdrop(backdropEl);   // optional; click closes
drawer.attachCloseButton(closeBtnEl); // any number of these
drawer.attachTitle(headingEl);       // wires aria-labelledby
drawer.attachDescription(pEl);       // wires aria-describedby
```

## Reactive accessors

```js
drawer.open()       // boolean
drawer.status()     // "closed" | "opening" | "open" | "closing"
drawer.side()       // "left" | "right" | "top" | "bottom"
```

## Mutations

```js
drawer.show() / .hide() / .setOpen(b, reason)
drawer.setSide("left")
```

## Painted attributes

### Content (attachContent)
- `role="dialog"` (modal) or `role="region"` (non-modal)
- `aria-modal="true"` (modal only)
- `aria-labelledby` (when title attached)
- `aria-describedby` (when description attached)
- `data-side="left|right|top|bottom"`
- `data-status="closed|opening|open|closing"`
- `data-open` (when open)

### Trigger (attachTrigger)
- `aria-haspopup="dialog"`
- `aria-expanded="true|false"`
- `aria-controls="<content.id>"`

### Backdrop (attachBackdrop)
- `data-drawer-backdrop`
- `data-open` (when open)
- `data-status` (mirrors content status)

## Lifecycle while open

When the drawer opens, the primitive (synchronously):
1. Portals the content + backdrop to `portalRoot` (defaults to
   `document.body`).
2. Locks body scroll (if `modal` + `lockScrollOnOpen`).
3. Saves the currently-focused element for restoration on close.
4. Installs a focus trap inside the content (if `modal` + `trapFocus`).
5. Binds the global Escape stack + outside-click dismiss.
6. Sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger.

On close it unwinds in reverse order. If `awaitTransitionEnd: true`,
the close completes only after the `transitionend` / `animationend`
event fires on the content -- so the slide-out animation gets to play
to completion before the DOM is detached.

## Custom element

```html
<lite-drawer side="right">
    <button data-drawer-trigger>Open filters</button>
    <div    data-drawer-backdrop></div>
    <aside  data-drawer-content>
        <h2 data-drawer-title>Filters</h2>
        <p  data-drawer-description>Refine your results.</p>
        <button data-drawer-close aria-label="Close">×</button>
        <!-- consumer content -->
    </aside>
</lite-drawer>
```

### Host attributes
- `side="left|right|top|bottom"`     -- reactive
- `open`                              -- reactive; mirrors drawer.open()
- `non-modal`                         -- disables backdrop + focus trap + scroll lock
- `no-escape`                         -- disables Escape-key dismiss
- `no-outside-close`                  -- disables outside-click + backdrop dismiss
- `await-transitions`                 -- wait for transitionend before closing

### Host API
```js
host.isOpen / .side / .status     // accessors
host.show() / .hide() / .setSide(s) / .setOpen(b)
host._drawerInstance
```

### Custom element events
- `openchange` `{ detail: { open, reason } }`

## Composition

- **Filter panel** in a data table: drawer holds combobox + slider +
  toggle-group filters; clicking the table's "Filter" button opens it.
- **Mobile nav**: drawer with `side="left"` containing a tree/menu;
  hamburger toggles it.
- **Row detail**: in a table, clicking a row opens a drawer with that
  row's details and edit controls.
- **Settings**: app-level settings panel that slides from the right.

## Notes

- Multiple drawers per page are supported. The escape stack handles
  N drawers correctly -- the most recently-opened drawer closes first
  on Escape.
- For animation: use CSS transitions on `[data-side]` + transform
  translate. Example:
  ```css
  [data-side="right"] { transform: translateX(100%); transition: transform 200ms; }
  [data-side="right"][data-open] { transform: translateX(0); }
  ```
- The primitive does NOT manage the slide-in CSS itself -- it only
  emits state attributes. This keeps the headless contract intact:
  consumer styles, primitive coordinates.
- Backdrop is optional. Without one, the drawer floats above the page
  with no overlay; outside-click + Escape still work if enabled.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
